continue: Recovery is genuinely trending up. Your HRV weekly average climbed 47 → 55 ms (“Balanced” every day) while weekly stress fell 30 → 23. Whatever shifted the last 2–3 weeks, keep it.
Resting HR is excellent and stable (46–53, 7-day avg 49) — a strong aerobic-fitness signal. Keep easy days truly easy (Zone 2) so this stays low.
Last week’s sleep was strong (85 / 94 / 89 / 82). Protect that bedtime — it’s clearly feeding the high readiness scores.
Training load sits in the optimal band (ACWR 1.1, 47%). You’re absorbing the work, so it’s safe to keep progressing ~10%/week.
change: Your polarized split is inverted — over the last 30 days only 22% of your miles were truly easy while 52% sat in the gray zone (avg HR ~139–151) and 27% hard. Polarized training wants ~80% easy / ~20% hard with almost no gray. This is the #1 fix and the likely root of the VO2max plateau: slow your easy runs to HR <135 so they actually recover you — which lets your hard days go genuinely hard. The gray-zone miles are tiring you without building base or driving adaptation.

VO2max has plateaued near 53 (precise 52.8) for ~6 weeks. That’s exactly why Thursday’s 5×3-min intervals exist — but it takes 4–6 weeks of consistent weekly quality work to move. Hold one hard session per week and be patient.

Garmin flags “Aerobic Low Shortage” — you need more easy Z2 volume, not more intensity. Ramp weekly mileage toward the 20s–30s as planned; that aerobic base is what raises the VO2max ceiling alongside the intervals.

Sleep duration swings a lot (6.6 h → 8.3 h), and your lower-scoring nights (69, 72, 77) had less deep + REM and more awakenings. Aim for a consistent 7.5 h+ with a steady bedtime — consistency beats the occasional long night.
Vigorous days average 2.6/week vs Garmin’s target of 3. The structured weekly intervals will close that gap and nudge your fitness age (31.6) toward its achievable 30.5.

Weight is now current — logged 174.4 lbs on Jun 17 (BMI ~24.3, down from the stale 25.0). That’s ~11 lbs above your low-160s goal; the Fuel app’s ~2,300 cal / 150 g protein plan is built for a sustainable ~1 lb/week. Keep logging weigh-ins so BMI and fitness age keep tracking — Garmin recomputes fitness age within a day or so.
